title: Affected refinement post-back v1
description: Spec artifact for design.md §26 #13, §14.5.4 - blocks DAG stages 9b/9c. A build-graph adapter (runko-ci, runner-side only) may POST this alongside a Change's project-level affected computation (webhooks/webhook-envelope.schema.json's 'affected' object), never in place of it. run_everything=true on ANY engine failure (query error, timeout, unparseable output, version skew) per §14.5.3's fail-closed rule - there is no partial-success shape. Whether check-set policies key on 'targets' (opt-in) or stay project-scoped (default) is an org policy choice made elsewhere; this payload is additive information either way.

change_id.description: Stable Change-Id this refinement applies to (§7.4).

head_sha.description: Revision the engine query was evaluated at - hermetic-at-SHA is what makes this trust class 'declared, not inferred' (§13.3).

engine.description: Which engine produced this refinement (§14.5.4's engine matrix).

strategy.description: How the engine was consulted (§14.5.8). 'rdeps' (the default when absent - pre-§14.5.8 producers): reverse-dependency query over the changed files at head, additive/advisory only. 'snapshot_diff': the graph was evaluated at BOTH base and head and diffed - the strategy that may REPLACE a refinable-only run_everything escalation, runner-side, where nothing gates on the result.

universe_pattern.description: e.g. '//...'. Absent when run_everything is true and no query was attempted.

run_everything.description: True on ANY engine failure - query error, timeout, unparseable output, missing engine binary, version skew. Consumers MUST treat this identically to the platform's own affected.run_everything: fail closed to a broader run, never fail open.

failure_reason.description: Present only when run_everything is true because of THIS adapter (as opposed to the platform floor already being run_everything for unrelated reasons). Free text for humans/logs, not a machine-parsed enum - failure modes here are engine-specific and open-ended.

targets.description: Affected target labels (e.g. 'commerce/checkout:go_default_test'), engine-native format. Empty when run_everything is true, or when the query genuinely found no affected targets.

target_projects.description: Optional target-label -> Runko project-name mapping, resolved via the same longest-path-prefix rule affected.Compute uses for changed paths (§14.5.4 item 5). Omitted when the adapter did not attempt project resolution; absence does not imply run_everything.

examples.failure_reason: bazel query: exit status 1: ERROR: no targets found beneath ''
